content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's opening move (turn 2) is the exact inversion the breakthrough required — refusing to engage the epistemological puzzle and flipping to 'how do you not trust yourself.' When the client intellectualizes back, the coach doesn't argue or repeat; pivots to what's scary (turn 4), notices the move-to-methodology pattern in real time (turn 6), and follows the emotional thread (what did the decisions cost you, turn 8). Each move is matched to the read: when client retreats to abstraction, coach gently names it; when client opens slightly, coach asks what's underneath. Turn 12's 'you're working very hard' is well-timed paradoxical-resistance work. Turn 14 is the critical hold — when the client finds 'the watching, the waiting to be wrong,' the coach doesn't move on, doesn't interpret, just says 'stay with that.' That's the right move at the right moment, and it lands (turn 15 produces the dissolution). Sequence integrity is strong: coach never offers a diagnostic or framework despite client's repeated invitations to do so, which would have been the trap. Moves are AoA-shaped (inversion, naming the intellectual-with-emotional-body, staying with), not generic.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opening inversion (how-do-you-not-trust-it) refuses the epistemological frame immediately — perfectly matched to the read."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pivots from methodology to fear — locates the emotional body under the intellectual question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the move-to-methodology in real time without judgment; redirects to the scary thing about this specific decision. Good pattern interruption."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the fear accurately ('instrument feels broken') then asks what it cost — going toward the value/loss rather than away."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names that the search itself is the exhausting thing, and offers the dissolving question (what if there isn't a method) — paradoxical-instruction-to-resist territory."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the working-hard quality and offers another angle when previous move was deflected. Coach pivots rather than repeats."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Critical move: holds the moment when client finds 'the watching, waiting to be wrong.' Doesn't fill silence, doesn't interpret — invites her to stay. This is where the question dissolves."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "lets-go-there", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist", "resistance", "how-do-you-not-trust-it", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "the-happiness-filter"],
  "notes": "The coach didn't explicitly ask 'what would shift most if it shifted,' but the inversion in turn 2 plus the staying-with move in turn 14 accomplished the same dissolving function. The happiness-filter wasn't named, though the composure was gently worked around rather than colluded with."
}
